channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New Orleans Parish assistant DA dies by apparent suicide in office: report
item.description: Ian Kersting, a 34-year-old Parish prosecutor near New Orleans, was found dead with "apparent self-inflicted gunshot wounds" at his office, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man behind New Orleans attack visited the city twice before: FBI
item.description: Shamsud-Din Jabbar, who was identified as the man shot and killed by police after the deadly New Year’s Day attack, traveled to New Orleans in late October and in November.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KREX 5

item.title: Fox Corp. supports New Orleans terror victims and family through Love One Louisiana Foundation
item.description: Fox Corp. is donating to the Love One Louisiana Foundation, providing support to victims and families of the New Orleans terror attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The FBI says the man responsible for New Year's truck attack visited New Orleans twice before
item.description: Shamsud-Din Jabbar, a U.S. citizen from Houston, also traveled to Cairo, Egypt, as well as Ontario, Canada, before the attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Feds reveal chilling new timeline of how maniac terrorist scoped out New Orleans kill zone
item.description: The FBI on Sunday released new details about the travels of 42-year-old Shamsud-Din Jabbar, the man authorities say carried out the deadly New Years' terrorist attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Data company figures out which airlines are most on time
item.description: Mexican airline Aeromexico had the world's best record for on-time arrivals in 2024, according to an annual ranking released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why you shouldn't charge your phone to 100%, according to experts
item.description: It may be tempting to charge to 100%, but experts say 80% may be enough — and beneficial for your phone. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tech law in 2025: a look ahead at AI, privacy and social media regulation under the new Trump administration
item.description: Artificial intelligence harms, problematic social media content, data privacy violations – the issues are the same, but the policymakers and regulators who deal with them are about to change.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Lu, University of Michigan, Fox 5

item.title: Opening a savings account? Here's what to know
item.description: Annual percentage yields (APYs) on top high-yield savings accounts are currently 4.5% and above, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Higher Social Security payments coming for millions of people from bill that Biden signed
item.description: President Joe Biden has signed into law a measure that boosts Social Security payments for current and former public employees.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NYC subway burning suspect, Laken Riley’s killer among illegal immigrants accused of preying on Americans
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta, who allegedly set a woman on fire in the NYC subway, and Laken Riley's killer, Jose Ibarra, were among violent migrants arrested or convicted in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Joe Burrow and the Cincinnati Bengals keep their playoff hopes alive by edging the Steelers 19-17
item.description: Joe Burrow passed for 277 yards and a touchdown, Cade York kicked four field goals and the Cincinnati Bengals kept their slim playoff hopes alive with a 19-17 win over the floundering Pittsburgh Steelers on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drivers skid and crash as wintry mix grips central US before moving east
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — Road conditions were deteriorating Saturday in the central U.S. as a winter storm brought a mix of snow, ice and plunging temperatures, with forecasts calling for the dreaded combo to spread eastward in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Orleans mourns victims of truck attack with tearful vigil and celebration of life
item.description: New Orleans has held a vigil on Bourbon Street to mourn and celebrate the lives of those killed when a man rammed a pickup truck into a crowd, killing and injuring revelers celebrating the new year.
item.dc:creator: Jim Mustian, Jack Brook, Stephen Smith And Sara Cline, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: IRS reminds taxpayers in 24 disaster area states of 2025 deadlines
item.description: A tax filing extension for residents of two dozen states affected by disasters is coming to an end in 2025, the IRS reminded taxpayers Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, KRON4

item.title: Suspected illegal immigrant's fake bomb threat shuts down SC highway
item.description: A suspected illegal immigrant was arrested in South Carolina after officials say he made a fake bomb threat during a traffic stop which ultimately led to a section of the I-85 being shut down for nearly five hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KTLA 5

item.title: University of Michigan lab develops blood test to identify HPV-related cancers
item.description: Researchers at a University of Michigan lab have developed a “first-of-its-kind” blood test to detect head and neck cancers caused by human papillomavirus.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Rudy Giuliani insists he's not hiding assets at contempt hearing over $148 million judgment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani seemed to favorably impress a judge with three hours of testimony Friday at a contempt hearing as he insisted he's not hiding assets from lawyers trying to recover a $148 million judgment for two Georgia election workers. Judge Lewis J.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In-N-Out Burger is expanding again in 2025. Here's where
item.description: Irvine, California-based In-N-Out Burger is planning to bring its mustard-grilled burgers and spiritual fountain sodas to new cities and states in the coming years, with a handful of those restaurants confirmed across three states in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: GOP trust in election accuracy rises after Trump's victory: Survey
item.description: Trump has publicly questioned or aired inaccurate claims about the 2020 election results, leading many within his party to also contest the legitimacy of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KRON4

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: Thomas has agreed to follow updated requirements on reporting trips and gifts, including clearer guidelines on hospitality from friends.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Federal appeals court strikes down Biden net neutrality rules
item.description: A federal appeals court ruled this week the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) lacked the authority to restore certain net neutrality rules last year, handing a blow to FCC Democrats and Biden administration officials who pushed for revived open internet measures.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: FBI releases new video of suspect planting pipe bomb on eve of Capitol riot
item.description: A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing it again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden says he will block US Steel sale to Nippon Steel
item.description: President Biden announced Friday he would block the sale of U.S. Steel to Japanese-owned Nippon Steel following a yearlong review of the potential acquisition. “We need major U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: 2 dead and 19 injured in Southern California plane crash
item.description: FULLERTON, Calif. (AP) — Two people died and 19 were injured Thursday when a small plane crashed through the roof of a sprawling furniture manufacturing building in Southern California where at least 200 people were working, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden awards the 2nd highest civilian award to leaders of the Jan. 6 committee and 18 others
item.description: President Joe Biden on Thursday awarded the second highest civilian medal to Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Downtown Omaha loses power for hours after a garbage truck gets stuck in a sinkhole
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A large portion of downtown Omaha lost power for several hours Thursday after a garbage truck got stuck in a sinkhole where utility lines had become exposed. The truck became stuck around 8 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI releases new video of a suspect planting a pipe bomb near DNC offices on eve of the Capitol riot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol? Hoping to generate new tips from the public, the FBI is releasing
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, ABC 4
